title:
EEG: Electrophysiological biomarkers of behavioral dimensions 
from cross-species paradigms
description: This dataset comprises electrophysiological recordings from 57 human participants performing three behavioral tasks designed to identify cross-species biomarkers of behavioral dimensions. The study employs EEG to characterize electrophysiological signatures that may translate across species, with data collected between 2014-2016 and analyzed through 2021. The research aims to establish objective neurophysiological markers relevant to behavioral phenotyping.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 57 human participants performing three different tasks. Trigger signals were encoded as odd binary recombinations and subsequently translated to 0-255 values using custom MATLAB scripts. Data collection occurred in San Diego (2014-2016), with subsequent analysis performed in New Mexico (2015-2021).
